Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Government Bonds, And Changing Venue And Approving Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Oct-07-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Jennifer Steenberg appeared for and with Star Romero (Mother) by zoom and for and with Martin Romero (Father/Guardian Ad Litem) by zoom.

The Court stated that the challenge in this matter is that the trust that is before it looks a lot like a Special Needs Trust ("SNT"), but it's not a SNT, that it is very unusual and hard to fit into a conceptual framework, and that it can do a blocked account, or a guardianship of the estate both of which are very restrictive as far as access to the funds. The Court also stated that until the more recent declaration of Mr. Romero it had before it an issue of an injury to the eye with no known treatments expected, and that now there is mention of a brain injury and behavioral issues. Ms. Steneberg stated there are no expected expenses for Amaro's vision and they do not expect any further expenses for medical issues and therefore, the funds do not need to be preserved and that they feel early interventions will allow Amaro to improve. The Court questions why not a SNT and what the thinking was behind this specific structure of the settlement. Ms. Steneberg stated that while Amaro is a minor he will not be eligible for public assistance, that when he turns 18 they do not know if he will need public assistance, that if he does, they will modify the trust, that the plan is to manage his issues while he is a minor, that this trust becomes revocable by Amaro at age 18 and that she was not involved with the structure of this. Ms. Romero stated that they got legal advice every step of the way, that they reviewed a lot of different options, that research shows that investing time and special attention at a younger age can allow the brain to create new/other pathways and that they want to provide as much care and attention at Amaro's early life. Ms. Romero also stated that they are hoping and praying that Amaro's brain can create new pathways and he is able to go to college, that they want him to have every available opportunity to go to college and that they want to make sure his funds are preserved in the event he needs long term care. The Court stated that the other challenge is that it is not comfortable with binding the other Court, that it wants the other Court to administer and supervise as it sees appropriate, that it is inclined to do preschool for one binding year and that they can then go to Alameda. The Court also stated that it is still concerned with Article Three Section 3 of the trust regarding major expenditures because it doesn't give specific instruction to the Trustee that any expenditure is not to exceed $2,500.00 and needs to be approved by the Court, and stated that Article Seven Section 6 regarding the Attorney's fees will boil down to whatever the Court approves. Mr. Romero thanked the Court for listening, stated that they appreciate the Court's consideration, that they understand that the funds are to be used only for Amaro and are to be preserved as much as they can for Amaro, and that it is their responsibility to provide everyday care and support for Amaro. The Amended Petition for Establishment of Minor's Settlement Trust, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Order Directing Establishment Of Minor'S Settlement Trust, Authorizing Investment In Mutual Funds And Government Bonds, And Changing Venue And Approving Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Continued To Sep-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Steneberg appeared by zoom for and with Martin Romero by zoom.
Star Romero (Mother of the Beneficiary) also appeared by zoom.

Discussion was held regarding the terms of the trust, the issue of the settlement funds not being intended for ordinary care and support of the Minor, the parents responsibility to provide for the Minor, the issue of what expenditures are allowed without a Court order and which ones aren't, the issue of power of appointment being given to a three year old, the parents inability to pay for the tuition and tuition being an appropriate use of the trust funds, the beneficiary receiving a better education, the issue of the funds being available to the Minor when he reaches the age of majority, the issue of venue being in Alameda County or Contra Costa County, Mr. Thomas' rate being at $145.00 per hour, etc. The Court stated that it needs more information regarding support of the Minor in writing and under oath, that it needs proof of the Trustee having an office in Alameda and Contra Costa, and asked Ms. Steneberg to think about and look at the spend thrift clause and the scope of it. The Petition for Order Directing Establishment of Minor's Settlement Trust, etc. is continued to September 29, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Everything is to be filed and courtesy copies are to be delivered by September 22, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
